architecture.summary: FlashMem distills latent memory from a frozen LLM backbone's transient hidden states without auxiliary encoders. A Shared-KV Consolidator attends directly to the backbone's frozen KV cache to synthesize memory representations; a parameter-free Cognitive Monitor uses attention entropy to adaptively trigger consolidation when epistemic uncertainty is high. Information flows from the backbone's reasoning trajectory into a consolidated memory vector that persists across interaction steps, eliminating re-parameterization overhead.
trained_components: Shared-KV Consolidator (attends to frozen backbone KV cache)

biblio.title: FlashMem: Distilling Intrinsic Latent Memory via Computation Reuse

claims.statement: FlashMem reduces inference latency by 5 times compared to heavy baselines
FlashMem matches the performance of heavy baselines while reducing inference latency
The last hidden state is identified as a sufficient statistic for the interaction history
Internal representations uniquely encode input trajectories, enabling memory distillation without auxiliary encoders
A parameter-free Cognitive Monitor leverages attention entropy to adaptively trigger consolidation only when high epistemic uncertainty is detected
